source: Compiled from public data center reports, SEC filings, corporate sustainability reports, and industry databases

datacenters.name: Ashburn / Loudoun County
datacenters.notes: Largest data center market globally. ~70% of world internet traffic routes through Loudoun County.

datacenters.name: Manassas, VA
datacenters.notes: Secondary NoVA cluster south of Ashburn. AWS has major campus here.

datacenters.name: Sterling, VA
datacenters.notes: Dense cluster adjacent to Ashburn corridor along Route 28.

datacenters.name: Prince William County, VA
datacenters.notes: Southern expansion of the NoVA data center corridor. Rapid permitting.

datacenters.name: Richmond, VA
datacenters.notes: Emerging secondary market south of NoVA. Lower land costs.

datacenters.name: Dallas / Fort Worth
datacenters.notes: Major interconnection hub. ERCOT grid — isolated from eastern/western interconnects.

datacenters.name: Fort Worth, TX
datacenters.notes: Western DFW cluster. Meta has large campus. Lower density than east Dallas.

datacenters.name: Midlothian, TX
datacenters.notes: Southern DFW expansion. Cheaper land, good power availability.

datacenters.name: San Antonio, TX
datacenters.notes: NSA Texas Cryptologic Center nearby. Rackspace HQ. Military-adjacent demand.

datacenters.name: Austin, TX
datacenters.notes: Growing tech hub. Samsung, Tesla, Apple campuses drive local demand.

datacenters.name: Houston, TX
datacenters.notes: Energy sector proximity. Hurricane risk. Major subsea cable landing area.

datacenters.name: Phoenix / Mesa
datacenters.notes: Rapid growth market. Cheap land, but extreme heat demands heavy cooling.

datacenters.name: Scottsdale, AZ
datacenters.notes: Eastern Phoenix metro. Premium location for enterprise colo.

datacenters.name: Goodyear, AZ
datacenters.notes: Microsoft mega-campus in West Valley. Significant water usage controversy.

datacenters.name: Chicago
datacenters.notes: Major financial exchange hub. CME and CBOE proximity. Cold climate aids cooling.

datacenters.name: Elk Grove Village, IL
datacenters.notes: Northwest Chicago suburbs. Dense industrial park with fiber access.

datacenters.name: Aurora, IL
datacenters.notes: Western Chicago metro. Growing cluster with new builds.

datacenters.name: San Jose / Santa Clara
datacenters.notes: Tech HQ proximity. Cleaner grid via CA renewables mandate. Constrained power.

datacenters.name: Santa Clara, CA
datacenters.notes: Equinix's largest US campus. Dense enterprise interconnection.

datacenters.name: San Francisco, CA
datacenters.notes: Urban core facilities. Limited expansion room. Premium pricing.

datacenters.name: Sacramento, CA
datacenters.notes: Overflow from Silicon Valley. CA clean energy grid. State government demand.

datacenters.name: Los Angeles, CA
datacenters.notes: Entertainment and media sector. Major Pacific subsea cable landing.

datacenters.name: San Diego, CA
datacenters.notes: Defense and biotech sector demand. Border proximity for US-Mexico connectivity.

datacenters.name: New York City Metro
datacenters.notes: Financial services hub. Constrained power and space. Premium colo market.

datacenters.name: Newark / NJ
datacenters.notes: NYC overflow. Fiber hub for transatlantic cables. Lower cost than Manhattan.

datacenters.name: Secaucus, NJ
datacenters.notes: Meadowlands corridor. Low-latency access to NYSE and Wall Street.

datacenters.name: Atlanta
datacenters.notes: Southeast hub. Connected via major north-south and east-west fiber routes.

datacenters.name: Douglasville, GA
datacenters.notes: QTS mega-campus west of Atlanta. Former printing plant conversion.

datacenters.name: Portland / Hillsboro
datacenters.notes: PNW hydro power access. Cool climate. No state sales tax advantage.

datacenters.name: The Dalles, OR
datacenters.notes: Google's first custom-built data center. Cheap Columbia River hydro power.

datacenters.name: Prineville, OR
datacenters.notes: Meta and Apple mega-campuses. Rural setting, low cost, clean hydro grid.

datacenters.name: Quincy, WA
datacenters.notes: Cheap Columbia River hydro. Remote location in arid Columbia Basin.

datacenters.name: Seattle
datacenters.notes: AWS and Microsoft HQ proximity. Clean hydro-heavy grid. Mild climate.

datacenters.name: Moses Lake, WA
datacenters.notes: Adjacent to Quincy cluster. Extremely cheap hydro power.

datacenters.name: Denver
datacenters.notes: Growing market. Cool dry climate at altitude. Mountain west fiber hub.

datacenters.name: Cheyenne, WY
datacenters.notes: Wind energy access. Extremely cold winters aid cooling. Tax incentives.

datacenters.name: Columbus, OH
datacenters.notes: AWS and Google investment hub. Central location, major fiber crossroads.

datacenters.name: New Albany, OH
datacenters.notes: Hyperscaler mega-campus hub northeast of Columbus. Rapid expansion zone.

datacenters.name: Las Vegas
datacenters.notes: Switch SuperNAP mega-campus. Solar potential but extreme water stress and heat.

datacenters.name: Reno / Sparks, NV
datacenters.notes: Switch Citadel campus and Apple facility. Geothermal energy potential.

datacenters.name: Boston
datacenters.notes: Biotech and pharma computing hub. Relatively clean New England grid.

datacenters.name: Minneapolis
datacenters.notes: Cold climate natural cooling advantage. Healthcare and finance demand.

datacenters.name: Miami
datacenters.notes: Latin America connectivity hub. NAP of the Americas is key interconnection point.

datacenters.name: Des Moines, IA
datacenters.notes: Major hyperscaler campus location. Wind energy access via Iowa grid.

datacenters.name: Council Bluffs, IA
datacenters.notes: Google's first Iowa campus. Adjacent to Omaha fiber hub. Wind-heavy grid.

datacenters.name: Omaha, NE
datacenters.notes: Central US fiber crossroads. Growing hyperscaler market. OPPD utility.

datacenters.name: Papillion, NE
datacenters.notes: Meta mega-campus south of Omaha. Wind-powered commitments.

datacenters.name: Charlotte, NC
datacenters.notes: Banking and finance sector demand. Duke Energy grid. Growing market.

datacenters.name: Raleigh-Durham, NC
datacenters.notes: Research Triangle tech cluster. Apple campus nearby. Moderate climate.

datacenters.name: Nashville, TN
datacenters.notes: Healthcare IT hub. TVA power — mix of nuclear, hydro, and coal.

datacenters.name: Salt Lake City, UT
datacenters.notes: Growing market. NSA Utah Data Center nearby. Mountain west fiber route.

datacenters.name: Pittsburgh, PA
datacenters.notes: Carnegie Mellon AI research adjacency. Affordable power. Cool climate.

datacenters.name: Philadelphia, PA
datacenters.notes: PJM Interconnection HQ region. Financial and healthcare demand.

datacenters.name: Indianapolis, IN
datacenters.notes: Central Indiana fiber hub. Salesforce and other tech employer demand.

datacenters.name: Kansas City, MO
datacenters.notes: Google Fiber city. Central US location. Historically low natural disaster risk.

datacenters.name: St. Louis, MO
datacenters.notes: Central US interconnection. Ameren utility. Boeing and defense demand.

datacenters.name: Detroit, MI
datacenters.notes: Automotive industry edge computing demand. Great Lakes cooling potential.

datacenters.name: Grand Rapids, MI
datacenters.notes: Switch Pyramid mega-campus. Lake Michigan cooling access.

datacenters.name: Buffalo, NY
datacenters.notes: Cheap Niagara hydro power. Very clean grid. Cold climate.

datacenters.name: Albany, NY
datacenters.notes: Upstate NY clean grid access. State tax incentives for data centers.

datacenters.name: Jacksonville, FL
datacenters.notes: Southeast subsea cable landing. Growing Florida market.

datacenters.name: Tampa, FL
datacenters.notes: Central Florida tech corridor. Hurricane risk but growing demand.

datacenters.name: Pryor, OK
datacenters.notes: Google mega-campus in former munitions plant. Low-cost power and land.

datacenters.name: Tulsa, OK
datacenters.notes: Energy sector hub. Low cost of operations.

datacenters.name: Birmingham, AL
datacenters.notes: Alabama Power service territory. Low electricity rates.

datacenters.name: Huntsville, AL
datacenters.notes: NASA, military, and defense computing. Google fiber campus. TVA power.

datacenters.name: New Orleans, LA
datacenters.notes: Gulf Coast connectivity hub. Hurricane and flood risk. Subsea cable landings.

datacenters.name: Little Rock, AR
datacenters.notes: Central Arkansas fiber crossroads. Windstream HQ. Low cost market.

datacenters.name: Memphis, TN
datacenters.notes: Logistics and FedEx demand. TVA power. Mississippi River cooling potential.

datacenters.name: Louisville, KY
datacenters.notes: UPS logistics hub adjacency. Moderate climate and power costs.

datacenters.name: Boise, ID
datacenters.notes: Emerging Pacific NW market. Idaho hydro power access.

datacenters.name: Spokane, WA
datacenters.notes: Eastern WA hydro power. Cool climate. Small but growing market.

datacenters.name: Albuquerque, NM
datacenters.notes: Sandia and Los Alamos lab adjacency. Solar potential. Extreme water stress.

datacenters.name: Tucson, AZ
datacenters.notes: University and defense demand. Solar potential. Severe water constraints.

datacenters.name: Waukegan, IL
datacenters.notes: Meta campus north of Chicago. Lake Michigan cooling proximity.

datacenters.name: Milwaukee, WI
datacenters.notes: Lake Michigan cooling. Midwest fiber routes. Cold climate advantage.

datacenters.name: Madison, WI
datacenters.notes: University of Wisconsin research computing demand.

datacenters.name: Sioux Falls, SD
datacenters.notes: No corporate income tax. Cold climate. Growing small market.

datacenters.name: Fargo, ND
datacenters.notes: Extremely cold climate natural cooling. Wind energy access. Small market.

datacenters.name: Hartford, CT
datacenters.notes: Insurance industry computing demand. New England clean grid access.

datacenters.name: Providence, RI
datacenters.notes: Offshore wind access potential. Small New England market.

datacenters.name: Virginia Beach, VA
datacenters.notes: Transatlantic subsea cable landing. Military demand. Flood risk.

datacenters.name: Sacramento (Rancho Cordova), CA
datacenters.notes: East Sacramento expansion. Lower cost CA location.

datacenters.name: El Paso, TX
datacenters.notes: US-Mexico border connectivity. Low cost. Extreme heat and water stress.

datacenters.name: Chandler, AZ
datacenters.notes: Major Phoenix expansion zone. Intel fab proximity.

datacenters.name: Lenoir, NC
datacenters.notes: Google mega-campus in rural NC. Former furniture factory region.

datacenters.name: Forest City, NC
datacenters.notes: Apple iCloud campus. Rural NC with incentive packages. Duke Energy grid.

datacenters.name: Maiden, NC
datacenters.notes: Apple's first major East Coast data center. 100% renewable claims.

datacenters.name: Northlake, IL
datacenters.notes: Western Chicago suburbs near O'Hare. Fiber density.

datacenters.name: Leesburg, VA
datacenters.notes: Northern edge of Ashburn corridor. Amazon expansion.

datacenters.name: Henrico County, VA
datacenters.notes: Meta campus outside Richmond. White Oak Technology Park.

datacenters.name: Bluffdale, UT
datacenters.notes: NSA Intelligence Community data center. Massive scale, classified workloads.

datacenters.name: North Las Vegas, NV
datacenters.notes: Northern LV expansion zone. Solar-adjacent but high water stress.

datacenters.name: San Marcos, TX
datacenters.notes: I-35 corridor between Austin and San Antonio. Emerging market.

datacenters.name: Boydton, VA
datacenters.notes: Microsoft mega-campus in rural southern VA. Low land cost. Dominion Energy grid.

datacenters.name: Lithia Springs, GA
datacenters.notes: QTS Atlanta-Metro campus. Former warehouse district conversion.

datacenters.name: Bridgeport, CT
datacenters.notes: NYC overflow market. Submarine cable proximity. New England grid.

datacenters.name: Clarksville, VA
datacenters.notes: Adjacent to Boydton Microsoft campus. Rural Virginia expansion.

datacenters.name: Plano, TX
datacenters.notes: North Dallas telecom corridor. AT&T and major carrier POPs.

datacenters.name: Carrollton, TX
datacenters.notes: Northwest Dallas metro. Established colo cluster along I-35E corridor.
